description:
Advances: specled.expanded_behavior_tier

Deliverable:
SpecLedEx.Realization.ExpandedBehavior reads :beam_lib.chunks(beam, [:debug_info]), extracts post-expansion AST per declared MFA, canonicalizes via SpecLedEx.Realization.Canonical.normalize/1, and hashes deterministically (term_to_binary [:deterministic, minor_version: 2]); emits detector_unavailable: :debug_info_stripped on modules compiled with @compile {:no_debug_info, true}; SpecLedEx.Realization.Typespecs hashes sorted @spec/@type declarations per MFA/type; mixed-debug-info fixture expansion (test/fixtures/sample_project/lib/sample_project/no_debug.ex with @compile {:no_debug_info, true}); other sample_project modules retain default debug_info.

Verification:
- Tests to pass:
  - mix test test/specled_ex/realization/expanded_behavior_test.exs
  - mix test test/specled_ex/realization/typespecs_test.exs
- Behaviors to demonstrate:
  - specled.expanded_behavior_tier.scenario.expanded_hash_stable_on_rename
  - specled.expanded_behavior_tier.scenario.no_debug_info_degrades
  - specled.expanded_behavior_tier.scenario.typespec_arg_change_drifts

Allowed touches:
- lib/specled_ex/realization/expanded_behavior.ex
- lib/specled_ex/realization/typespecs.ex
- test/specled_ex/realization/expanded_behavior_test.exs
- test/specled_ex/realization/typespecs_test.exs
- test/fixtures/sample_project/lib/sample_project/no_debug.ex
- test/integration/ (mixed-debug-info integration test only)

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/specled_ex/realization/use.ex (S5b)
- lib/specled_ex/realization/api_boundary.ex (S2)
- lib/specled_ex/realization/implementation.ex (S3b)
- lib/specled_ex/realization/closure.ex (S3b)
- lib/specled_ex/realization/drift.ex (S5b extends, not here)
- lib/specled_ex/compiler/**
- lib/specled_ex/coverage/**

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do NOT extend Compiler.Context schema or HashStore schema here; if expanded_behavior needs new fields, flag in PR (S5a and S3a are constrained to not run in parallel for this reason).
- Reuse SpecLedEx.Realization.Canonical unchanged; do NOT fork an expanded-AST canonicalizer.
- Typespecs hashing sorts declarations lexicographically before hashing to prevent order-induced drift.
- Missing debug_info must degrade gracefully (detector_unavailable), never raise.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly
- [ ] Tests listed above pass
- [ ] Mixed-debug-info fixture green
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

Summary:
ExpandedBehavior.hash/2 pulls `:beam_lib.chunks(beam, [:debug_info])` and extracts the elixir_v1 `module_map` via `backend.debug_info/4`, then canonicalizes the matching MFA's clauses through the existing SpecLedEx.Realization.Canonical (no fork, per Out-of-Scope intent) and hashes with the deterministic term_to_binary+SHA-256 pipeline. Typespecs.hash/2 pulls per-MFA `@spec` declarations via Code.Typespec.fetch_specs/1 and module-level `@type` declarations via Code.Typespec.fetch_types/1, strips typespec-form line metadata recursively, and sorts declarations on their serialized form before hashing so source-order swaps cannot induce drift. Both tiers emit detector_unavailable (reason: debug_info_stripped) once per module on the stripped path — collapsing duplicate bindings — and never raise. Modern Elixir only strips Elixir debug_info via `@compile {:debug_info, false}`, not `{:no_debug_info, true}` (the Erlang-side Abst-chunk option); the fixture sets both so the beam is fully stripped across supported versions. `mix spec.check` shows 0 findings on specled.expanded_behavior_tier; the 3 remaining warnings are pre-existing specled.use_tier baseline (S5b / q59.8 scope). Compiler.Context schema and HashStore schema unchanged per Out-of-Scope intent.

These are S5b / q59.8 scope — pre-existing baseline left intact by this ticket. 0 findings attributed to specled.expanded_behavior_tier.
- Full test suite: 373 tests, 0 failures (mix test --include integration)

Dual-attribute fixture evaluation: CONFIRMED — the implementer's claim is factually correct on Elixir 1.18/OTP 27. Reproduced via elixirc of four single-attribute vs dual-attribute modules and :beam_lib.chunks(bin, [:debug_info]):
  * @compile {:no_debug_info, true} alone -> debug_info chunk PRESENT ({:debug_info_v1, :elixir_erl, <data>}) // NOT stripped
  * @compile {:debug_info, false} alone -> STRIPPED ({:debug_info_v1, :elixir_erl, :none})
  * both attributes -> STRIPPED (matches fixture)
  * default -> PRESENT
The fixture uses both so it honors the spec's literal wording (@compile {:no_debug_info, true}) while actually producing a debug_info-less beam. This is a pragmatic correctness choice, not a spec-weakening change.

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- The SpecLedEx.Realization.Binding.resolve/2 beam-first rule from specled.decision.beam_first_binding_resolution is not directly invoked by ExpandedBehavior.hash/2; instead the tier does its own Code.ensure_loaded -> :beam_lib.chunks -> backend.debug_info(:elixir_v1, ...) walk. This is consistent with the decision's scope (resolve/2 is the module-level source/beam resolver for arbitrary MFA text, whereas expanded_behavior goes straight to debug_info by design). No concern, but flagging for auditor awareness.
- Unit tests rely on Kernel.ParallelCompiler.compile_to_path/2 after forcing Code.put_compiler_option(:debug_info, true) because ExUnit runs can leave the global compiler option flipped. Reasonable workaround; documented inline.
- "warning: redefining module ..." noise in ExpandedBehaviorTest + TypespecsTest is expected (fixtures recompile the same module names across scenarios) and does not reflect a defect.
- Umbrella degrade path exercised via ExpandedBehaviorTest run/3 umbrella graceful degrade and mirrored in Typespecs — consistent with specled.decision.umbrella_deferred_v1_1.

AUDIT: BOUNCE
Session: auditor-q59.7-parent-session
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/ (parent conversation)

Reasoning: Mechanical scope clean (8 files, all Allowed; 0 Out-of-Scope; Canonical/HashStore/Compiler.Context untouched; zero raise on stripped path verified; dual-attribute fixture independently confirmed correct via :beam_lib.chunks against OnlyNoDebug vs OnlyDebugFalse vs Both vs default on Elixir 1.18 — only {:debug_info, false} strips the Elixir debug_info chunk). 5 of 6 must requirements have tests that would fail if the behavior were broken. The hash_stable_on_refactor requirement (expanded_behavior_test.exs:181, "hash is stable when the DSL-emitted body is only cosmetically renamed") does NOT exercise α-rename on expanded AST: the test hashes SpecLedEx.ExpBehTest.Consumer.handle_event/2, but the rename x -> y is applied to def plain(x) in the Consumer, whose AST is never in the hash input (find_definition only pulls clauses matching {fun, arity} for the target MFA). The DSL-emitted body is byte-identical across @dsl_baseline and @dsl_renamed. The test therefore passes purely on strip_meta of line metadata — it would pass even if Canonical.alpha_rename were a no-op. I independently confirmed that renaming DSL-body locals event/state -> evt/st DOES flip the hash (ffa6d89... -> 2fab735...) because Canonical\'s α-rename deliberately skips ctx not in [nil, Elixir] and the expanded DSL-body variables carry ctx = DSL module name plus counter metadata. The test as written cannot detect an α-rename regression on expanded AST.

If BOUNCE, specify for whom:
- BOUNCE -> implementer: hash_stable_on_refactor test must hash an MFA whose clause AST actually contains the renamed variable. Option A (cleanest): hash SpecLedEx.ExpBehTest.Consumer.plain/1 instead of handle_event/2 across @dsl_baseline vs @dsl_renamed — plain/1 is source-context (ctx=nil post-expansion, confirmed), so α-rename will actually run on it and the test will guard the requirement. Option B: keep handle_event/2 but add a second assertion hashing plain/1 across the same fixtures. Either way, do NOT modify Canonical (Out of Scope). The scenario\'s literal wording ("local-variable rename in the DSL\'s emitted body") is unsatisfiable with Canonical\'s current narrow α-rename scope; update the spec scenario comment to clarify that rename-stability is tested against source-context code extracted from debug_info, consistent with specled.decision on Canonical\'s α-rename scope.
- BOUNCE -> verifier: missed that the test named for hash_stable_on_refactor passes for an orthogonal reason. The verifier\'s note "also :217 confirms real semantic change flips" proves the hash IS responding to the DSL body — but it does not prove the hash is stable under rename of the DSL body (which it isn\'t, independently verified). Walking the test code would have shown that Consumer.plain/1 is never the hash target.

IMPLEMENTED (iteration 2)
Session: bf605248-e6bf-4e28-9a32-110d2cfd8d12
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/bf605248-e6bf-4e28-9a32-110d2cfd8d12.jsonl

Commit: e456fd2

Audit bounce → implementer accepted. Fix landed in two parts:

1. New test `hash is stable across local-variable rename in source-context code`
   (test/specled_ex/realization/expanded_behavior_test.exs:221) hashes
   `Consumer.plain/1` across baseline (`def plain(x), do: x + 1`) and
   renamed (`def plain(y), do: y + 1`) fixtures. `plain/1`'s bound
   variable carries `ctx = nil` post-expansion (source-defined, not
   macro-emitted), which is the case Canonical's α-rename DOES collapse.
   The test would fail if α-rename were a no-op. Old `handle_event/2`
   test renamed to "DSL-emitted handle_event/2 hash is stable when
   surrounding source is reflowed" — it remains a meaningful sanity
   check (an unrelated MFA's hash should not move when the target MFA's
   AST is byte-identical), but is no longer claiming to test α-rename
   on macro-hygiene variables (which Canonical deliberately preserves).

2. Surfacing #1 exposed a wrapping bug: the prior 4-tuple
   `{:__expanded__, fun, arity, clauses}` blocked `Macro.prewalk`
   (used by Canonical) from reaching the inner clause AST — prewalk
   only recurses through 3-tuples and 2-tuples. Fix: normalize each
   clause individually inside `find_definition/3` and wrap AFTER
   normalization for the per-MFA hash input. Plus: added a private
   `deep_strip_meta/1` mirroring `Implementation.deep_strip_meta/1`
   verbatim — Canonical's `strip_meta` only recurses into the args
   position, not the form position, so `{:., [line: N], [:erlang, :+]}`
   metadata on remote-call dot expressions survives. Same pre-existing
   limitation that `Implementation` worked around. Canonical untouched.

Verified locally:
- mix test test/specled_ex/realization/expanded_behavior_test.exs → 12 tests, 0 failures
- mix test test/specled_ex/realization/typespecs_test.exs → 8 tests, 0 failures
- mix test --include integration → 374 tests, 0 failures
- mix spec.check → 0 expanded_behavior_tier findings; 3 pre-existing
  use_tier (q59.8 scope) warnings, baseline.

Files touched in this iteration (both Allowed):
  - lib/specled_ex/realization/expanded_behavior.ex
  - test/specled_ex/realization/expanded_behavior_test.exs

VERIFY: PASS
Session: subagent-verify-q59.7-iter2
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/ (subagent session — parent conversation log)

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched (cumulative b5c0278..e456fd2): 8
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0
  (.spec/specs/expanded_behavior_tier.spec.md + .spec/state.json accepted under S3b/S4a/S4b precedent: own-subject status draft->active, three execute: false->true flips on own-subject stubs, and pure regeneration of findings/strength entries. No substantive spec-text edits. Iteration-2 diff is scoped strictly to lib/specled_ex/realization/expanded_behavior.ex and test/specled_ex/realization/expanded_behavior_test.exs — both Allowed.)

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 3, passed: 3
  * mix test test/specled_ex/realization/expanded_behavior_test.exs -> 12 tests, 0 failures
  * mix test test/specled_ex/realization/typespecs_test.exs -> 8 tests, 0 failures
  * source_file test/fixtures/sample_project/lib/sample_project/no_debug.ex exists, carries `# covers: specled.expanded_behavior_tier.mixed_fixture_coverage` (state.json: strength=linked)
- Requirements with passing tests: 6/6
  * reads_beam_debug_info -> expanded_behavior_test.exs:154 "returns {:ok, binary} for a function that lives only in expansion" (hashes DSL-emitted handle_event/2 which has no source defn; 32-byte SHA-256 + deterministic repeat)
  * hash_stable_on_refactor -> expanded_behavior_test.exs:221 "hash is stable across local-variable rename in source-context code" (NEW, fixes the bounce). Hashes Consumer.plain/1 across baseline (def plain(x), do: x + 1) and renamed (def plain(y), do: y + 1). See α-rename verification below. :181 "DSL-emitted handle_event/2 hash is stable when surrounding source is reflowed" (repurposed) + :258 "hash flips when the DSL-emitted body changes semantically" round out the property (reflow-stable + change-detecting).
  * no_debug_info_detector_unavailable -> expanded_behavior_test.exs:294 (hash/2 returns {:error, {:debug_info_stripped, _}}) + :308 (does not raise) + :327/:357 (run/3 emits detector_unavailable once per module, never branch_guard_realization_drift)
  * typespecs_hashes_spec_and_type -> typespecs_test.exs:118 (32-byte binary) + :152 (reorder-invariant across @spec/@type declarations)
  * typespecs_drift_finding -> typespecs_test.exs:178 (integer()->binary() flips the hash) + :215 (run/3 emits branch_guard_realization_drift tier=typespecs); :132 proves whitespace-only reformat does NOT flip
  * mixed_fixture_coverage -> test/integration/mixed_debug_info_fixture_test.exs:56/63/69/75 (Sample hashes, SampleProject.NoDebug degrades on both tiers, collapse-to-one finding)
- α-rename actually-exercised verification: CONFIRMED via runtime probe
  1. Independently confirmed via elixirc + :beam_lib.chunks that Consumer.plain/1's bound variable carries ctx = nil (not a DSL-module ctx). Exact AST node: {:x, [version: 0, line: 2, column: 13], nil}. This matches Canonical.rename_node/2's guard `ctx in [nil, Elixir]` (canonical.ex:117), so α-rename WILL collapse x and y.
  2. Ran a differential probe (/tmp/probe_alpha.exs) mirroring ExpandedBehavior.find_definition/3's new normalize_clause pipeline:
     * WITH α-rename (current impl): hash_a == hash_b (d4e264f4b5df4d85...). Test passes for the RIGHT reason.
     * WITHOUT α-rename (simulated no-op, only deep_strip_meta): hash_a = ee5c4ddb..., hash_b = 44926159.... DIFFER. Test would FAIL.
  3. The wrapping-bug fix is load-bearing: {:__expanded__, fun, arity, clauses} is a 4-tuple, opaque to Macro.prewalk. Normalizing each clause BEFORE wrapping (normalize_clause/1 inside find_definition/3) ensures Canonical.alpha_rename actually reaches the inner variable nodes.
  4. Private deep_strip_meta/1 in expanded_behavior.ex:275-295 is byte-identical to Implementation.deep_strip_meta/1 (implementation.ex:376-392). Same known Canonical.strip_meta limitation (form-position meta on remote-call dot expressions survives) — not a Canonical fork, just an identical local workaround already precedented in Implementation. Canonical left untouched.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none
  * lib/specled_ex/realization/canonical.ex diff b5c0278^..e456fd2: 0 lines. Untouched.
  * lib/specled_ex/compiler/** diff: 0 lines. Schema unchanged.
  * lib/specled_ex/realization/hash_store.ex diff: 0 lines. Schema unchanged.
  * ExpandedBehavior still reuses Canonical (expanded_behavior.ex:73-74 hash input wraps Canonical.hash(...); normalize_clause pipes into Canonical.normalize/1).
  * Typespecs sorts lexicographically before hashing (typespecs.ex:238-242).
  * Neither tier raises on stripped path (confirmed via :308 "does not raise" + zero raise sites). Tagged-tuple returns throughout.
  * Deterministic hashing ADR honored (canonical.ex:62, typespecs.ex:241: [:deterministic, minor_version: 2]).
- Project verification (mix spec.check): exits non-zero with 3 warnings. 0 findings attributed to specled.expanded_behavior_tier. All 3 warnings target specled.use_tier (surface_target_missing on lib/specled_ex/realization/use.ex + two use_tier test files) — q59.8 scope, pre-existing baseline, unchanged by this ticket.
- Full test suite: 374 tests, 0 failures (mix test --include integration)

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- The auditor's bounce was surgical and correct. Iteration-2 addresses both the detection issue (the new plain/1 test actually exercises α-rename) AND a latent wrapping bug the auditor's counter-example surfaced: the old 4-tuple {:__expanded__, fun, arity, clauses} wrapper was opaque to Macro.prewalk (which only recurses through 2- and 3-tuples). Prior to the fix, α-rename would not have reached inner clause vars even if the test had targeted the right MFA. Normalizing each clause individually before wrapping fixes both.
- The old handle_event/2 test was retained (renamed, expanded comment) as a reflow/sibling-edit sanity check. It is no longer misattributed as testing α-rename on macro-hygiene variables — which Canonical deliberately preserves, per canonical.ex:11-14 / canonical.ex:117. The spec scenario text (expanded_hash_stable_on_rename) speaks to "local-variable rename in the DSL's emitted body" — the implementer is honoring the requirement's INTENT (cosmetic refactors must not drift) via source-context code where Canonical's α-rename scope actually operates, rather than a misframed scenario that would silently regress if macro-hygiene collapsing were ever added. Reasonable pragmatic read consistent with Canonical's documented scope.
- Warning noise ("redefining module ...") from fixture recompilation in ExpandedBehaviorTest + TypespecsTest is expected and does not indicate defect.
- No new detector raises; stripped path goes through {:error, {:debug_info_stripped, mod}} consistently.

AUDIT: APPROVE
Session: auditor-q59.7-iter2-bouncefix-audit
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/ (parent conversation log)

Reasoning: Iteration-2 correctly fixes the orthogonality gap that earned the prior bounce. I ran my own independent α-rename probe (/tmp/audit_probe.exs — compiled AuditProbe.A with `def plain(x), do: x + 1` and AuditProbe.B with `def plain(y), do: y + 1`, pulled debug_info via :beam_lib.chunks, applied ExpandedBehavior's normalize_clause pipeline verbatim, and hashed). Results: WITH α-rename both fixtures hash to d4e264f4b5df4d85bd6b7c65eef1cee482d2050c38a5593796af5fe1082962d7 (identical — matches the verifier's reported hash exactly); WITHOUT α-rename (simulated no-op, only deep_strip_meta) the hashes diverge to ee5c4ddb... and 44926159... — proving the new test at expanded_behavior_test.exs:221 would FAIL if α-rename were a no-op. I independently confirmed the variable node shape for `plain/1` is {:x, [version: 0, line: 2, column: 13], nil} and {:y, [version: 0, line: 2, column: 13], nil} — ctx=nil, which satisfies Canonical.rename_node/2's guard `ctx in [nil, Elixir]` (canonical.ex:117). The wrapping-bug fix is real and load-bearing: find_definition/3 now normalizes each clause individually (expanded_behavior.ex:258-263) BEFORE wrapping into the 4-tuple, so Canonical.alpha_rename actually reaches inner variable nodes (Macro.prewalk only recurses into 2- and 3-tuples). The private deep_strip_meta/1 at expanded_behavior.ex:281-297 is byte-identical to Implementation.deep_strip_meta/1 at implementation.ex:376-392 — not a Canonical fork, just a local mirror of the same precedented workaround. Canonical.ex diff b5c0278^..e456fd2 is empty (0 lines). All 6 must requirements have passing tests that would fail if the behavior were broken. Mechanical scope: 8 files cumulative, all Allowed; iteration-2 diff scoped to expanded_behavior.ex + expanded_behavior_test.exs (both Allowed). Spec.md edits match q59.4/q59.5/q59.6 precedent (status draft->active + 3 execute false->true on own-subject stubs, no substantive spec-text edits). Test runs: expanded_behavior_test 12/0, typespecs_test 8/0, full suite 374/0. mix spec.check: 3 pre-existing specled.use_tier warnings (q59.8 scope), 0 specled.expanded_behavior_tier findings. No Compiler.Context or HashStore schema changes.

If BOUNCE, specify for whom: N/A (APPROVE)

title: S5a: expanded_behavior + typespecs tiers
